Article Title: 'Hope for productive monsoon session': PM Modi highlights India's gains ahead of Parliament proceedings
Article Text:
"Country has achieved numerous milestones, a series of accomplishments", PM Modi said ahead of Monsoon session.

NEW DELHI: Prime Minister Narendra Modi Monday addressed the nation ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, expressing optimism for the productive proceedings, which is most likely to be dominated by the issues of donations theft at Ayodhya Ram Mandir , NEET paper leak and the delimitation bill."Be it the monsoon or the Monsoon Session - if both are proactive, they prove to be highly productive. And when both are productive, it leads to the welfare of the nation and the well-being of all living beings. That is why we pray that the monsoon remains both proactive and productive, and that the monsoon session proves productive as well," PM Modi said.Taunting Rahul Gandhi in a veiled attack, PM Modi praised the succcessful mission of Vikram-1 orbital launch vehicle highlighting how the average age of the young Skyroot team is merely 28 years, unlike a "56-year-old youth.""India's youth have embarked on a new journey into space. I was told that the average age of the entire team working at the 'Skyroot' startup is merely 28 years. Young people like these have accomplished this feat. I am not speaking of a 56-year-old youth," PM Modi said.He futher said that the country has "achieved numerous milestones, a series of accomplishments that fill every citizen with pride"."Be it on the national stage, the international arena, or now, even in space, these have truly been moments of pride for India.Just before the monsoon session last year, an Indian citizen reached the International Space Station. And just the other day, a young Indian startup achieved a monumental feat".PM Modi said that a few countries in the world have witnessed such private enterprise," adding that "India's youth have embarked on a new journey into space. It is a tremendous success... India's global profile is gaining universal recognition and acceptance. This is no coincidence; it is a message, a powerful message that the potential and aspirations of our country's youth are as boundless as space itself."PM Modi fruther expressed concern over the West Asia war, sayaing that it posed a major crisis for countries like India that rely on others for energy."Numerous obstacles and crises arose regarding petrol, diesel, LPG, fertilisers, and chemicals. Despite this, India maintained a growth rate of 7.7 per cent, emerging as the fastest-growing major economy in the world. This reflects India's inherent strength and offers a glimpse of the enthusiasm and zeal with which the nation aspires to scale new heights. It is against this backdrop that the Monsoon Session is commencing; the country has gathered momentum, and the spirit of Parliament infuses fresh energy into that pace," he said.He also mentioned Rajasthan's oil refinery and the hydrogen train, praising the achievement as a "testament to the dedication of India's scientists, engineers, innovators, entrepreneurs, and workers, all of whom are living and working with a shared goal for the nation.""It is the result of the country boarding the Reform Express that India's youth are able to show such great courage and achieve success. Recently, a massive oil refinery was dedicated to the nation in Rajasthan. Just a few weeks ago, a semiconductor plant, the third one, was dedicated to the country. Not long ago, a green hydrogen-powered train was dedicated to the citizens; very few countries in the world possess this facility," the PM said.The Monsoon Session of Parliament commences on Monday, with the Lok Sabha scheduled to introduce The Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026, besides taking up obituary references, Question Hour, papers to be laid on the Table, a ministerial statement, and other listed business, according to the Lok Sabha's List of Business.Under legislative business, Union Minister Arjun Ram Meghwal will move for leave to introduce the Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Amendment Bill, 2026, which seeks to further amend the Supreme Court (Number of Judges) Act, 1956. The Bill will replace the ordinance promulgated by the Centre to increase the sanctioned strength of judges in the Supreme Court from 33 to 37.The lower House will also take up a motion regarding the Joint Committee on Offices of Profit, to be moved by BJP MPs Eatala Rajender and Ajay Bhatt.The motion recommends that the Rajya Sabha elect two of its members to the Joint Committee on Offices of Profit through the system of proportional representation by means of the single transferable vote to fill the vacancies caused by the retirement of NR Elango and Deepak Prakash, and communicate the names of the elected members to the Lok Sabha.
Article Summary: "country has achieved numerous milestones, a series of accomplishments", pm modi said ahead of monsoon session. Monsoon session of parliament commences on monday, with the lok sabha scheduled to introduce the supreme court (number of judges) amendment bill, 2026.

Article Title: 'They came very late': CJP claims govt reached out, but 'no clarity' on talks
Article Text:
"The administration is trying to open talks with the government," CJP spokesperson said.

NEW DELHI: Cockroach Janta Party ( CJP ) Monday claimed that the government tried to reach out to them through the administration (police) ahead of the Parliament march and that the party was willing to talk with the dispensation with "open hearts"."The administration is trying to open talks with the government. They have come very late, but we have opened our hearts and said that we are willing to talk to them. There has been no clarity from their side as of now. The ball is in their court," CJP spokesperson Saurav Das told news agency PTI.He further said: "Sonam ji, so far he is stable, and he is continuing his hunger strike. He has sent his very best wishes to the young protesters of this country who have finally awakened to this very difficult, very dead system that does not listen to them at all. A peaceful march will happen very soon. Peaceful protesters have been gathered here for the past 30 days, and we have shown how to carry out a peaceful protest and put our points forward. "A member of activist Sonam Wangchuk 's legal team said on Sunday that Wangchuk's condition remained stable and confirmed that he was continuing his hunger strike. The team also said Wangchuk had conveyed his best wishes to young protesters supporting the campaign and reiterated that a peaceful march would be held soon.Earlier in the day, Wangchuk said he would end his indefinite fast if the Centre accepted responsibility for recent failures in the education system, including alleged paper leaks, or if Members of Parliament assured him that the issue would be raised during the Monsoon Session beginning on Monday.In a post on X, Wangchuk listed three conditions under which he would call off his hunger strike."I will end my fast on 20th July if: a. If the govt takes accountability of the recent failures in Education system, Paper leaks etc or b. If I and the leadership of CJP reach the doorsteps of Parliament where MPs and leaders of various parties assure us that they will now take up the issue in the Parliament or c. If my health or other factors do not permit this then the MPs and leaders of different parties visit this Hospital and give give the the above assurance . ..From the illegal detentions at Safdarjung Hospital, where my freedom of movement, speech and communication are restricted."Meanwhile, supporters gathered at Jantar Mantar ahead of the proposed 'Chalo Sansad' march to Parliament on Monday. Addressing the gathering, CJP president Abhijeet Dipke said the march would go ahead despite restrictions."The biggest march in India's history is about to take place. For the past 10-12 years, attempts have been made to destroy democracy in this country. By sitting at Jantar Mantar for a month, you have revived democracy," Abhijeet Dipke said.Dipke said the month-long sit-in had demonstrated the determination of the protesters and claimed supporters were arriving from different parts of the country, filling roads leading to the protest site. Delhi Police , however, issued a public advisory stating that no permission had been sought or granted for any march to Parliament by the CJP. The police warned that strict action would be taken against anyone violating prohibitory orders in the New Delhi district.In its advisory, Delhi Police said prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), corresponding to the former Section 144 of the CrPC, remain in force across the district.The advisory said protest marches, processions, demonstrations and gatherings of five or more people are prohibited, except at the designated protest site at Jantar Mantar with prior permission.Police added that elaborate security arrangements had been put in place ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament, which begins on July 20, to ensure public safety, protect key installations and maintain security in the national capital.Delhi Police also warned that anyone found violating the prohibitory orders would face prosecution under Section 223 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and other applicable provisions of law.
Article Summary: "the administration is trying to open talks with the government," cjp spokesperson said. Delhi police issued a public advisory stating that no permission had been sought or granted for any march to parliament by the cjp. The police warned that strict action would be taken against anyone violating prohibitory orders in the new delhi district.

Article Title: First Cholesterol-Lowering Pill to Replace PCSK9 Injections Gets US Nod
Article Text:
(Representative image) US FDA approves world's first oral PCSK9 inhibitor (Photo: IANS).

NEW DELHI: Patients with difficult-to-control high cholesterol could soon have an oral alternative to powerful cholesterol-lowering injections after the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) approved the world's first oral PCSK9 inhibitor, a class of medicines that dramatically lowers "bad" cholesterol.The once-daily tablet, Lipfendra (enlicitide), has been approved for adults with high cholesterol, including those with an inherited condition called heterozygous familial hypercholesterolaemia (HeFH). The drug is to be used along with diet, exercise and existing cholesterol-lowering treatment. Until now, PCSK9 inhibitors have only been available as injections administered every two or four weeks. However, the drug is not expected to be available in India immediately."Even if a medicine is approved abroad, it cannot be marketed in India automatically. Besides patent-related restrictions, it must obtain approval from the CDSCO after following the prescribed regulatory process, which may include clinical trials as required under Indian regulations. In exceptional cases, patients may seek permission through a separate CDSCO mechanism to import limited quantities for personal treatment.However, medicines cannot simply be brought into the country by friends or relatives," said Dr Subhash Mandal, national general secretary, Indian Pharmaceutical Association (IPA).The approval is significant because many high-risk patients continue to have elevated low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol despite treatment with standard oral medicines. Indian cardiology guidelines recommend aggressive LDL reduction, with targets below 70 mg/dL for high-risk individuals and below 55 mg/dL for those at very high cardiovascular risk, but many patients fail to achieve these goals despite multiple therapies.With cardiovascular disease remaining India's leading killer, cardiologists say better LDL cholesterol control is key to preventing heart attacks and strokes. Clinical trials involving 3,207 adults found the once-daily oral drug reduced LDL cholesterol by 56-59% over 24 weeks in patients already on maximally tolerated statins, delivering reductions comparable to injectable PCSK9 inhibitors and offering another option for those whose cholesterol remains uncontrolled despite existing therapy.However, cardiologists cautioned that the drug is unlikely to replace statins or become a first-line treatment."PCSK9 inhibitors have so far been available only as injections every two or four weeks. The new oral therapy lowers LDL, or 'bad' cholesterol, by about 50-60%, making it a promising option for patients who prefer to avoid injections," said Dr Ambuj Roy, Professor of Cardiology at AIIMS Delhi.He said the drug is likely to be used as a fourth-line therapy, after statins, ezetimibe and bempedoic acid, for patients whose LDL cholesterol remains uncontrolled or who cannot tolerate statins. "While it lowers LDL substantially, we still lack long-term evidence that it reduces heart attacks, bypass surgery, stenting or deaths. It is also expected to be expensive," he said.High LDL cholesterol is a major risk factor for heart disease and stroke. It causes fatty plaques to build up inside arteries, increasing the risk of heart attacks and strokes.
Article Summary: Us fda approves world's first oral pcsk9 inhibitor. Once-daily tablet, lipfendra (enlicitide), has been approved for adults with high cholesterol. Drug is to be used along with diet, exercise and existing cholesterol-lowering treatment.

Article Title: 'Misleading the families?' AI-171 victims' kin seek black box data, memorial after AAIB affidavit
Article Text:
Air India AI-171 crash

NEW DELHI: The families of those killed in the Air India AI-171 crash have stepped up demands for the release of the aircraft's black box data and cockpit voice recorder (CVR) transcript, questioning the aircraft accident investigation bureau's (AAIB) decision to withhold the material while promising only a final investigation report.According to news agency PTI, the demands were raised at a meeting of victims' families in Ahmedabad on Sunday, days after the AAIB informed the Supreme Court that it would not disclose black box data or cockpit voice recorder recordings during the ongoing investigation.The London Gatwick-bound Air India flight AI-171 crashed shortly after take-off from Ahmedabad's Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport on June 12, 2025, when the Boeing 787 Dreamliner struck the BJ Medical College hostel complex. The crash killed 241 of the 242 people on board and 19 people on the ground, making it India's deadliest aviation disaster in decades.Romin Vahora, who lost his brother, niece and aunt in the tragedy, questioned the AAIB's decision to withhold the cockpit recordings. "If they never intended to release the data, why did they release a cockpit voice recording transcript (as part of preliminary report) just a few days after the crash?", he asked.Vahora alleged that the families wanted to know whether the selective disclosure was intended to "shift the blame onto the pilots or mislead the victims' families", PTI reported.Seeking greater transparency, he said the families wanted the raw black box data to be made public, shared directly with them or provided to their legal representatives.Vahora said the families had not initiated legal proceedings and were awaiting the final investigation report, while their lawyers continued exploring legal options to secure access to the black box data. He added that if the report was not released within the timeline indicated by the AAIB, the families would consider holding protests after obtaining the necessary permissions.Last week, the AAIB told the Supreme Court that the draft final report into the June 12 crash was expected to be ready in October. In its affidavit, the agency said there was an "absolute statutory prohibition" on releasing cockpit voice recordings and airborne image recordings, arguing that witness statements, cockpit voice recorder transcripts, air traffic control communications and medical records must remain confidential to protect the integrity of the investigation and future aviation safety inquiries.The victims' families also renewed their demand for a permanent memorial at the crash site. Vahora urged the government, Air India and the Tata group to earmark land and allocate funds for a monument honouring those who lost their lives.
Article Summary: Families of those killed in the air india ai-171 crash have stepped up demands for the release of the aircraft's black box data and cockpit voice recorder (cvr) transcript. The demands were raised at a meeting of victims' families in ahmedabad on sunday. The families also renewed their demand for a permanent memorial at the crash site.

Article Title: CJP’s 'Sansad Chalo' march: Thousands gather at Jantar Mantar amid tight security — top developments
Article Text:
AAP leaders reach at Jantar Mantar

CJP activists say ready for dialogue with government, await clarity

Police use mild force to disperse CJP protesters

Gates reopened at temporarily shut Metro stations

Night-long vigil called as crowds swell

Dipke's fast enters day 3

Wangchuk's wife says hunger strike can end on one condition

Wangchuk lays down three conditions to end fast

NEW DELHI: Thousands of Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) supporters gathered at Jantar Mantar on Sunday ahead of the party’s proposed ‘Chalo Sansad’ march to Parliament, prompting heightened security measures across the national capital. Delhi Police denied permission for the rally and deployed additional personnel at key locations, including Jantar Mantar and areas surrounding Parliament House.The security arrangements were intensified ahead of the first day of the Monsoon Session, with police maintaining a strict vigil at the protest site amid expectations of a large turnout. Authorities said the party had not obtained official approval for the march.Delhi Police stated that prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (formerly Section 144 of the CrPC) continue to remain in force across New Delhi. The restrictions bar unauthorised gatherings, rallies and the carrying of weapons, especially in sensitive zones such as Parliament House and India Gate during the ongoing parliamentary session.Here are the top developments:Senior Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) leaders, including Manish Sisodia, Sanjay Singh and Atishi, arrived at Jantar Mantar to participate in the Parliament march.Earlier Manish Sisodia had appealed to people across political affiliations to join the proposed march to Parliament, urging citizens to stand in solidarity with the country's youth.CJP activists said they are open to holding talks with the government, while maintaining that they are awaiting clarity from the authorities on the proposed dialogue.Speaking to ANI, CJP activist Saurav Das said, “The administration is trying to open talks with the government. They have come very late, but we have opened our hearts and said that we are willing to talk to them. There has been no clarity from their side as of now. The ball is in their court.”Delhi Police used mild force on Monday to disperse CJP protesters attempting to march towards Parliament House from Jantar Mantar. Security personnel intervened as hundreds of demonstrators moved ahead, causing traffic disruptions. Rapid Action Force personnel were deployed to maintain order and manage the situation during the Parliament session.The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) earlier on Monday closed Janpath, Rajiv Chowk, Patel Chowk, Central Secretariat and Seva Teerth metro stations until further notice due to security concerns. However, shortly after the closure, DMRC announced that interchange facilities and gates at Rajiv Chowk and Central Secretariat stations had been reopened.Late on Sunday, banners, slogans and swelling crowds transformed Jantar Mantar into a packed protest camp after CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke called for a night-long vigil ahead of Monday's proposed Parliament march.The gathering came as supporters continued their sit-in, demanding accountability over alleged irregularities in the NEET-UG 2026 examination and paper leak controversy.CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke entered the third day of his indefinite hunger strike on Monday, continuing his protest after Sonam Wangchuk was removed from the demonstration site on Saturday. He has asserted that the march will go ahead and has demanded Prime Minister Narendra Modi's resignation.Amid the growing crowds, Sonam Wangchuk's wife, Gitanjali J Angmo, said the activist would end his hunger strike if political leaders visited him at Safdarjung Hospital and assured him that the issue of education accountability would be raised during Parliament's Monsoon Session. Her statement came a day after Wangchuk was shifted to hospital following a medical examination.On Monday, Angmo also moved the Delhi High Court against a single-judge order refusing her plea to shift Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital to a private hospital of his choice.In a post on X on Monday, Wangchuk said he would call off his hunger strike only if the government or political leaders accepted accountability over the education crisis.Earlier on Sunday, Wangchuk had described the planned march as: "India's 2nd FREEDOM MOVEMENT" He added that it was a movement for "freedom from injustice (like paper leaks)" and "freedom from fear (my illegal detention)".
Article Summary: Thousands of cockroach janata party (cjp) supporters gathered at jantar mantar on sunday ahead of the party’s proposed ‘chalo sansad’ march to parliament. Delhi police denied permission for the rally and deployed additional personnel at key locations.

Article Title: UN raises concern over unrest in PoK, seeks investigation into protest-related deaths
Article Text:
United Nations human rights agency voices concern over 'wave of unrest' in PoK (AP image)

Calls for investigation

How India reacted

Poll Vote & Share your view What is your opinion on the banning of the Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC)? It violates freedom of expression It's justified for public order 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

The United Nations human rights agency has expressed concern over the ongoing unrest in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK), urging authorities to conduct impartial investigations into the deaths of protesters and security personnel ahead of the region's legislative assembly elections.In a statement issued from Geneva on Friday, UN high commissioner for human rights Volker Türk appealed for calm as tensions continue to escalate ahead of the July 27 polls. According to the UN, dozens of people, the majority of them protesters, along with some law enforcement personnel, have reportedly been killed since June.Türk called for prompt, thorough and independent investigations into all deaths linked to the unrest. He also voiced concern over the banning of the Joint Awami Action Committee (JAAC), the civil society movement spearheading the protests, under Pakistan's anti-terrorism laws on allegations of threatening public order and security. Several leaders of the group have since been arrested.The UN rights office said criminalising a civil society organisation and imposing restrictions on public gatherings raise serious concerns over the rights to freedom of expression, peaceful assembly and association.It also urged authorities to ensure detained JAAC leaders have access to legal counsel, contact with their families, and full due process.The statement further criticised restrictions on internet access in the region. “We urge the authorities to ensure full internet access throughout the territory,” adding that Turk calls for meaningful and inclusive political dialogue to address the underlying issues and grievances of the local population.India, on July 14, said the ongoing protests in Pakistan-occupied Kashmir are the result of Pakistan's "decades-long systemic exploitation and denial of fundamental rights".The MEA accused Islamabad of responding to public dissent with excessive force instead of addressing people's grievances.The remarks were made by ministry of external affairs spokespersonRandhir Jaiswal during a media briefing, where he also responded to questions on several foreign policy issues, including the ongoing crisis in West Asia.Responding to a question on the protests in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir, Jaiswal said, "The ongoing protests in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir are a direct consequence of Pakistan's decades-long systemic exploitation, denial of fundamental rights and administrative operation in areas under its illegal and forcible occupation. Rather than addressing the legitimate grievances of the local population, the Pakistani state has responded with excessive police brutality... We expect and hope that the international community will hold Pakistan fully accountable for these egregious abuses and misdeeds."India has consistently maintained that Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir is an integral part of India and has repeatedly accused Pakistan of human rights violations and suppressing the rights of people living in the region.
Article Summary: United nations human rights agency voices concern over 'wave of unrest' in pok. Dozens of people, the majority of them protesters, along with some law enforcement personnel, have reportedly been killed since june. India, on july 14, said the ongoing protests in pakistan-occupied kashmir are the result of pakistan's "decades-long systemic exploitation and denial of fundamental rights"

Article Title: 'Rise in instability in Iran': Indian embassy sounds alarm; urges people to leave, postpone travel
Article Text:
'Rise in instability in Iran': Indian embassy sounds alarm; urges people to leave, postpone travel

NEW DELHI: The Indian Embassy in Iran on Sunday issued a revised travel advisory, urging Indian nationals to postpone all travel to the country.It also advised Indians already in Tehran to consider temporarily leaving the country using available commercial flights, as the security situation continues to deteriorate amid the escalating conflict in the Middle East.The advisory comes as military hostilities between the United States and Iran have intensified, with fresh strikes, retaliatory attacks and growing concerns over the security situation across the region.In its advisory, the embassy said, "The recent days have seen an increase in levels of instability and conflict in Iran."It advised Indian nationals planning to visit Iran 'for any purpose' to postpone their travel until the security environment improves.The embassy also urged Indians already in Iran to 'consider temporarily exiting Iran, using available flight options.'For those choosing to remain in the country, the mission advised exercising the highest level of caution, closely monitoring local developments and avoiding areas witnessing increased military activity, particularly along Iran's southern coast."Indian nationals who choose to remain in Iran should exercise the highest possible level of caution.They should carefully monitor the news to maintain full situational awareness, and should avoid locations seeing higher levels of military activity such as those along the southern coast of the country. Instructions issued by local authorities should be complied with carefully," the advisory read.The embassy also asked all Indian nationals in Iran who have not yet registered with the mission to do so immediately and regularly follow its official website and social media channels for further updates.The advisory was issued as the conflict between the United States and Iran escalated further on Sunday.According to Iranian authorities, the United States carried out fresh strikes on the under-construction Darkhovin nuclear power plant and other military targets, while the US Central Command (CENTCOM) said it targeted Iranian coastal surveillance systems, air defence networks, maritime assets and missile and drone facilities.Iran, meanwhile, announced retaliatory drone attacks against two US military installations in Kuwait and claimed to have intercepted a US MQ-9 drone over Ahvaz. It also warned that the Strait of Hormuz remained under the control of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC), amid heightened tensions over maritime security.The International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) said it was investigating reports of the attack on the Darkhovin nuclear facility and added that the site contained no nuclear material during its last inspection. The UN nuclear watchdog said the reported strike was not believed to pose any radiological risk but reiterated its call for military restraint around nuclear-related sites.The latest escalation has raised fresh international concern over the widening conflict in the Middle East as the war completes nearly five months.
Article Summary: The indian embassy in iran issued a revised travel advisory, urging indian nationals to postpone all travel to the country. It also advised indians already in tehran to consider temporarily leaving the country using available commercial flights. The advisory comes as military hostilities between the united states and iran have intensified.

Article Title: Even as fertility rates drop, young still aspire to partnership, parenthood, shows UN report
Article Text: NEW DELHI: A first-of-its-kind survey of young adults (aged 18-39) across 73 countries, including India, by United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) shows that even as much of the world’s marriage rates have declined and fertility has fallen to historically low levels, young people are not turning away from relationships or family life. Marriage, it says, remains “a common ideal relationship” arrangement among surveyed young adults.The report highlights that across countries — with very different demographic realities — many continue to aspire to partnership and parenthood, but say their decisions are increasingly shaped by financial security, stable employment, housing, gender equality and confidence in the future.UNFPA’s ‘Lives, Choices and Futures — Demographic Futures Survey’ 2026’ gathered perspectives of more than 1,08,000 internet connected young adults, including 1,722 respondents in India.More than two thirds of respondents selected an ideal relationship and living arrangement that includes marriage — 36% before living together and 34% after a period of cohabitation. The survey says 16% reported remaining single as their preferred living arrangement.Its findings gain significance, as across much of the world partnership formation is occurring later, marriage rates have declined and fertility has fallen to historically low levels.“Two thirds of the world’s population now lives in a country or area where the total fertility rate is below 2.1 children per woman — the level commonly associated with the population size remaining stable over time,” the survey says.Among respondents aged 25–39, who ideally wish to marry or live with a partner, a quarter are currently single and not dating. The proportion is higher among male respondents (30% than among female respondents (19%).Financial security is the highest-rated consideration for partnership formation, cited as important by 81% of respondents. It turns out that economic and housing constraints constitute the most commonly reported category of barrier to partnership, rated important by 57%.The survey finds that among respondents — currently seeking a partner — personal networks, school or work environments, and online platforms are the most commonly reported channels for meeting potential partners, each reported by approximately one third of respondents. Overall the survey says more than 40% of respondents report spending over two hours a day on social media or the Internet for entertainment, making it the most time-intensive form of online activity examined.In India, while more than eight in 10 respondents (83%) said they feel positive or very positive about the future, nearly half of those surveyed (47%) said they are very worried about conflict, economic insecurity and inequality.While across most regional groupings of countries, two children is the most commonly reported ideal family size, the mean number of children reported by respondents aged 35–39 is lower than the mean ideal number of children reported by respondents aged 18–39.For instance, in the case of India, women aged 18-39 reported an ideal family size of 2.1 children, compared with an average of one child among women aged 35-39. Men reported an ideal family size of 2.2 children, compared with an average of 1.1 children among men aged 35-39.Parenthood is part of the future plans of most surveyed in India. Among respondents aged 35-39 in India who do not currently have children, nearly 85% said they would ideally like to become parents.
Article Summary: Unfpa’s ‘lives, choices and futures — demographic futures survey’ 2026’ gathered perspectives of more than 1,08,000 internet connected young adults, including 1,722 respondents in india. More than two thirds of respondents selected an ideal relationship and living arrangement that includes marriage.

Article Title: 'Tear gas guns arrived': CJP says BJP 'goons', Delhi Police lurking to 'create mischief'
Article Text:
CJP posted a photo of a security personnel sitting with a tear gas gun at the protest site.

Poll Vote & Share your view Should the government respond more actively to protests like CJPs? Yes, they should engage. No, protests are often disruptive. 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

NEW DELHI: Cockraoch Janta Party (CJP) Sunday claimed 30-40 "goons" from the ruling BJP and the Delhi Police are lurking to "create mischieve" at the Jantar Mantar protest site, which remains tense after Sonam Wangcuk was removed on the Day 21 of his indefinite hunger.Further, citing the sources, the party of the activists claimed that an attempt can be made to raise anti-India provocative statements."Sources: 30-40 goons from BJP and Delhi Police have infiltrated our protest site and will try to make anti-India provocative statements to create mischief today. We have to expose and reject such people - they are not supporters of CJP or Sonam Wangchuk!" CJP posted on X.In another post, the CJP posted a photo of a security personnel sitting with a tear gas gun at the protest site.Earlier, Wangchuk described the proposed July 20 Parliament march as India's "second freedom movement", calling for "freedom from fear" and "freedom from injustice" while urging supporters to make the mobilisation a "big success".In a handwritten message shared from Safdarjung Hospital through his wife Gitanjali J Angmo, Wangchuk linked "freedom from injustice" to alleged paper leaks and "freedom from fear" to what he described as his "illegal detention".The note, shared on X through Wangchuk's account, read: "20th July. India's second freedom movement. Freedom from injustice (like paper leaks). Freedom from fear (my illegal detention)."It further said, "March to the Parliament. Please make it a big success."The note was signed off with the words, "Sent through Gitanjali from my illegal detention at Safdarjung."The reference to "illegal detention" appears to reiterate Wangchuk's allegation that he is being kept at Safdarjung Hospital against his wishes after being forcibly taken away from the Jantar Mantar protest site.The Cockroach Janata Party (CJP) has called for the July 20 Parliament march, demanding accountability over alleged irregularities in competitive examinations, reforms in the examination system and the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.Wangchuk, who joined the CJP agitation on June 28, was forcefully shifted to Safdarjung Hospital after his prolonged hunger strike at Jantar Mantar.
Article Summary: Cjp posted a photo of a security personnel sitting with a tear gas gun at the protest site. Sonam wangcuk was removed on the day 21 of his indefinite hunger. Wangchuk described the proposed july 20 parliament march as india's "second freedom movement"

Article Title: Modi speaks to Qatar Amir, recalls legacy of his father
Article Text: NEW DELHI: PM Narendra Modi spoke to Qatar Amir Sheikh Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani to convey his condolences on the passing of the latter's father Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, the Father Amir of Qatar.Recalling the Father Amir's significant contributions as the chief architect of modern Qatar, PM paid tribute to his visionary leadership and recalled his pivotal role in strengthening India-Qatar relations over the years as well as his deep affection for India and the Indian community in Qatar, according to an Indian readout."Recalled his deep affection for India, his warmth towards the Indian community in Qatar and his steadfast commitment to India-Qatar relations. His enduring legacy will continue to guide our two countries as we further strengthen our strategic partnership," Modi posted on X.India had earlier this week declared a day of national mourning on the passing of the Father Amir, acknowledging his contribution to the bilateral relationship. Qatar remains one of India's most important partners in the Gulf, as it is home to a sizeable Indian diaspora and also a principal source of energy, particularly LNG."The Amir of Qatar thanked the PM for his call and conveyed his appreciation for the words of support in this difficult hour. The two leaders reaffirmed their resolve to carry forward the Father Amir's legacy and further strengthen the India-Qatar strategic partnership and people-to-people ties," said Indian govt, adding that the leaders agreed to remain in close touch.
Article Summary: Pm narendra modi spoke to qatar amir sheikh tamim bin hamad al thani. Pm paid tribute to his visionary leadership and recalled his pivotal role in strengthening india-qatar relations over the years. India had earlier this week declared a day of national mourning on the passing of the father amir.

Article Title: Oil prices jump 3% as US-Iran conflict intensifies, Brent crude tops $90 per barrel
Article Text: Oil prices rallied on Monday after conflict between the United States and Iran intensified, raising fresh concerns over crude supplies from the Middle East as shipping through the Strait of Hormuz came under increasing pressure.Brent crude climbed above $90 a barrel, rising 2.30% to $90.13, while US benchmark WTI crude gained 2.07% to $84.20. The move extended last week's sharp rally, when Brent surged 15.9%, its biggest weekly gain since April and WTI rose 15.5%, its strongest weekly advance since early March.The latest gains followed another night of US military action against Iran. The US launched strikes for the ninth consecutive night, while Iran continued attacks across the region, with Bahrain and Kuwait reporting fresh missile and drone strikes.The growing hostilities have also disrupted movement through the Strait of Hormuz, a key maritime route that normally carries around one-fifth of global oil trade.The US said that it was enforcing a naval blockade on Iranian ports, while Iran has maintained that it is targeting ships that violate its navigation rules in the strategic waterway.Shipping activity through the strait has already slowed. LSEG data showed only four vessels passed through the route on Sunday, compared with eight a day earlier.Since Friday, three oil products tankers and one Very Large Crude Carrier have entered the strait to load crude.Adding to concerns, a vessel reportedly caught fire near Oman's coastline. The United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations agency reported the incident northwest of Kumzar and said the cause of the blaze was not immediately known. The British military also confirmed that a ship was on fire in the Strait of Hormuz.The US military has been urging commercial vessels to follow a route closer to Oman while transiting the strait. Iran, however, has responded by attacking ships using that route, insisting it should control navigation through the waterway."The coming days and weeks will provide a clearer picture of the sustainable level of oil exports from the region under renewed dual blockades," Barclays analyst Amarpreet Singh said in a note cited by Reuters."As things stand, we think oil markets are still too complacent about the potential fallout for inventories, which, unlike at the beginning of the war, are at the tightest of the past five years."Meanwhile, military conflict also widened.The US announced another round of strikes after confirming the death of another American service member, who it said was killed in Iraq during the "controlled detonation" of a downed Iranian drone. The military also said "unidentified remains" found on Sunday were being examined after one service member went missing following Friday's attack in Jordan. It said 17 US service members have been killed since the conflict began.In a statement, US Central Command said, "The strikes will continue degrading Iranian military capabilities used to attack commercial vessels and civilian mariners transiting the Strait of Hormuz."Iran, meanwhile, continued retaliatory attacks across the region. Bahrain, Jordan and Kuwait activated their air defences against incoming drones and missiles, while Israel warned that missiles fired towards Jordan could spill into Israeli territory.On the commodities front, escalating tensions have kept oil prices on an upward trajectory, leaving markets on edge. However, despite the recent rally, crude remains well below the peak of $126 a barrel reached during the early phase of the conflict.
Article Summary: Brent crude climbs above $90 a barrel, rising 2.30% to $90.13. Us benchmark wti crude gained 2.07% to £84.20. The move extended last week's sharp rally, when brent surged 15.9%, its biggest weekly gain since april.

Article Title: Revealed: The eye-watering amount Spain players will earn for winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup
Article Text:
Spain's Rodri (16) lifts the trophy as Spain celebrates after winning the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis)

How much each Spain player will receive

Argentina receive runners-up prize





Argentina walks on the pitch after their loss in the World Cup final soccer match between Spain and Argentina in East Rutherford, N.J., near New York, Sunday, July 19, 2026. (AP Photo/Julio Cortez)

Record prize money across the tournament



Champions - Spain: $51 million

Runners-up - Argentina: $34 million

Third place - England: $30 million

Fourth place - France: $28 million

Quarter-finalists (5th-8th): Switzerland, Norway, Belgium, Morocco: $20 million each

Round of 16 (9th-16th): Colombia, Egypt, United States, Portugal, Mexico, Brazil, Paraguay and Canada: $16 million each

Last 32 (17th-32nd): Ghana, Algeria, Australia, Cape Verde, Austria, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Senegal, DR Congo, Ecuador, Sweden, Ivory Coast, Netherlands, Germany, Japan and South Africa: $12 million each

Group stage (33rd-48th): Uruguay, Scotland, South Korea, Iran, Uzbekistan, Iraq, Saudi Arabia, Curaçao, Czech Republic, Qatar, Panama, Jordan, Tunisia, New Zealand, Haiti and Turkey: $10 million each

Spain's triumph at the 2026 FIFA World Cup did not only bring the nation's second world title. It also secured one of the biggest financial rewards ever distributed to a World Cup-winning squad after FIFA increased the tournament's prize money to record levels.Luis de la Fuente's side defeated Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the final at the New York New Jersey Stadium, with Ferran Torres scoring the decisive goal in the 106th minute. The victory earned the Royal Spanish Football Federation (RFEF) the tournament's top prize of $51 million from FIFA.According to reports, Spain's players will each receive a substantial share of that prize under a pre-agreed bonus arrangement negotiated between the squad captains and the RFEF.FIFA awards federations a single payment once their World Cup campaign concludes, with the amount determined by their final finishing position rather than after each knockout round.By merely reaching the World Cup final, Spain had already guaranteed the RFEF $34 million.Under the agreement reported by AS, 40 per cent of that guaranteed prize money would have been allocated to the players. That would have created a dressing-room bonus pool worth approximately $13.6 million, equating to just over $523,000 for each member of Spain's 26-man squad before taxes.Winning the World Cup, however, significantly increased those figures.After lifting the trophy, Spain's players became entitled to 45 per cent of FIFA's winners' prize.That means approximately $22.95 million will be shared between the 26 players, with each squad member expected to receive around $882,700 before taxes, according to AS. The remainder of FIFA's prize money will stay with the RFEF, which will use part of the windfall to cover the costs of Spain's World Cup campaign while also benefiting financially from one of the most lucrative tournaments in its history.Although Argentina fell short of retaining the World Cup after Ferran Torres' extra-time winner, Lionel Scaloni's side will still receive a substantial runners-up payment.FIFA will award the Argentine Football Association $34 million for finishing second.Unlike Spain, however, no official details have yet emerged regarding how that prize money will be distributed among Argentina's 26-player squad.The 2026 World Cup featured the largest prize fund in the competition's history, with FIFA increasing overall financial rewards by around 50 per cent compared with previous editions.Prize money based on final finishing positions is as follows:Those performance-based payments account for $703 million of FIFA's total $871 million tournament prize fund.The remaining $168 million is distributed among participating national associations regardless of where they finish, ensuring every nation receives additional financial support for taking part in football's biggest tournament.For Spain, however, victory delivered far more than just the World Cup trophy. Alongside a place in football history, every member of the squad is set to receive one of the largest bonuses ever awarded to a Spanish national team, reflecting both FIFA's record prize fund and the agreement reached between the players and the RFEF.
Article Summary: Spain's triumph at the 2026 fifa world cup secured one of the biggest financial rewards ever distributed to a world cup-winning squad. Fifa increased the tournament's prize money to record levels. Spain's players will each receive a substantial share of that prize under a pre-agreed bonus arrangement negotiated between the squad captains and the rfef.

BTS adds another FIFA milestone

first K-pop act to headline a FIFA World Cup Final Halftime Show

Dreamers

"a great honour"

Will BTS release a FIFA World Cup song?

"Mmm,"

BTS is preparing for what could be one of the biggest performances of their careers, as the group reunites on football's grandest stage. The septet will serve as headliners of the historic FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Halftime Show, the first halftime entertainment spectacle in a World Cup final. Millions of football fans and ARMY across the world are expected to tune in and viewers are now looking for the exact broadcast timings, streaming platforms and what to expect from the landmark event.The final of the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be played on Sunday 19 July at the New York New Jersey Stadium. Kickoff is set for 3:00 p.m. ET (19:00 GMT).The halftime show is expected to begin around 3:55 p.m. ET, though it could start a little earlier or later depending on stoppage time in the first half and delays to the game. The performance is expected to be about 11 minutes long and FIFA are expected to extend the usual half-time break to allow for stage production and artist performances, reports said.Fans around the world can watch the show through FIFA's official broadcast partners in their respective countries.The 2026 edition introduces a Super Bowl-style halftime show to the FIFA World Cup final for the very first time. FIFA has partnered with Global Citizen for the production, while Coldplay frontman Chris Martin is serving as the creative curator.BTS will share the stage with an impressive lineup that includesThe celebrations will begin even before kick-off. Post Malone is expected to lead the closing ceremony, while Jennifer Hudson is scheduled to perform the national anthem. Several other celebrities, including Tom Cruise, IShowSpeed, Robbie Williams, Nicole Scherzinger and Laura Pausini, are also expected to be part of the World Cup festivities.This performance marks BTS' first appearance together at a FIFA World Cup ceremony since completing military service and resuming full group activities. It also makes them theThe event carries added significance for Jungkook, who previously performedduring the FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022 opening ceremony. His appearance at the 2026 final makes him the first Korean and Asian artist to perform at multiple FIFA World Cup ceremonies.In an official statement, BTS reflected on the opportunity, saying it wasto perform on a stage that brings people together from across the globe. The group added that they believe music can inspire hope and unity, while supporting children's education through the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund makes the occasion even more meaningful.Speculation about new BTS music tied to the tournament continues to grow. During a Weverse Live on July 8, j-hope was asked directly if BTS would release a FIFA song. Instead of confirming or denying the possibility, he simply replied,before shrugging.That brief reaction was enough to send fans into overdrive. While no official announcement has followed, many ARMY members believe the group could still have another surprise planned as the World Cup final approaches.
Article Summary: Bts set to headline the historic fifa world cup 2026 final halftime show. Performance will take place during the july 19 final at new york new jersey stadium. Fans can watch it through official broadcasters worldwide, including zee5 in india. Event also features shakira, madonna, justin bieber and others.

Article Title: Jayden Adams' death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend is dragged into the drama days after his tragic passing
Article Text:
Jayden Adams' death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend is dragged into the drama days after his tragic passing.(Image via Getty Images)

Jayden Adams’ cause of death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend gets dragged into the drama

South African midfielder and winger, Jayden Adams, passed away last weekend leaving millions of fans in mourning. While initially there were rumors of Jayden Adams committing suicide due to the grief he caried over his grandmother’s death last month, things have now taken a serious turn.In the last few hours, as per News24’s recent report, Jayden Adams reportedly had an argument with his girlfriend and the mother of his child, Aqueelah Adendorf, just hours before he tragically passed away.While it is not known what exactly happened between Jayden Adams and his long term girlfriend, Aqueelah Adendorf, it is being alleged that he committed suicide shortly after.Minutes later, Jayden Adams reportedly texted his father, “I can’t take it anymore”.However, these chain of events have not ben confirmed by South African authorities yet as Gayton McKenzie, minister ⁠of sports, arts and culture in South Africa, has brutaly slammed this report.A few hours ago, Gayton McKenzie, minister ⁠of sports, arts and culture in South Africa, took to X and wrote, “This is irresponsible reporting, do you want the young lady now to take her own life ? Do you want her child to blame her for the passing of his dad?”He added, “You are truly a tabloid, this country needs serious newspapers for sure.Let’s allow the police to do their job.”Last week, Gayton McKenzie had also paid a tribute to Jayden Adams in an emotional statement as he wrote, “I shall carry forever the humble, ⁠appreciative response he gave me. That he chose to wear the national jersey and give his all for his country in that moment speaks to a depth of character and professionalism well beyond his years, ‌and it reflects the calibre of young man South Africa has lost…”However, neither Jayden Adams nor his family has responded t the speculations yet as the family tries to mourn his death in private.Interestingly, Jayden Adams’ girlfriend and the mother of his child, Aqueelah Adendorf, has also remained distant from the speculations as she posted a heartfelt tribute last week.Jayden Adams’ girlfriend, Aqueelah Adendorf, wrote, “There are no words to describe the pain I’m feeling. “Rest in peace, my love.”She added, “Thank you for every memory, every laugh, every hug, and every moment we shared. You were not only the love of my life but also my greatest supporter and my best friend.”At this point in time, it has not been confirmed how the 25 year old South African midfielder and winger, Jayden Adams, passed away last weekend as authorities remain involved in the high profile investigation.
Article Summary: Jayden adams' death takes a controversial turn as his girlfriend is dragged into the drama days after his tragic passing. South african midfielder and winger, jayden adams, passed away last weekend leaving millions of fans in mourning. It has not been confirmed how the 25-year-old died as authorities remain involved in the investigation.

Article Title: IShowSpeed’s father shares the unexpected 12-hour trip he took just to watch his son at the FIFA World Cup final
Article Text:
IShowSpeed’s father (Image Via Getty)

IShowSpeed’s father explains the long journey to reach the FIFA World Cup final

IShowSpeed’s World Cup appearance became even more special because of his father’s support

IShowSpeed had one of the biggest moments of his career before the 2026 FIFA World Cup final, but another person quietly stole hearts online. A video shared by @IShowSpeedHQ showed the streamer sitting with his father inside the stadium before the closing ceremony. During the conversation, his dad revealed that he had to take a 12-hour bus ride after bad weather canceled his flight, just so he could be there for his son on one of the biggest days of his life. FIFA had earlier confirmed that IShowSpeed was part of the World Cup final closing ceremony lineup.The short video begins with IShowSpeed sitting beside his father in the stadium before the ceremony. As they talked, Speed asked how his dad managed to reach the venue.His father replied with a full explanation, saying, “My flight got canceled because of the weather. I had to catch a bus, and I was on that bus for 12 hours. But I wasn’t going to miss this. I had to be here to support my son.”The moment quickly stood out because it was not about football or the performance. Instead, it showed the effort a parent was willing to make for his child.Travel plans often change because of bad weather, but his father decided not to give up.Rather than waiting for another flight, he chose a much longer trip by bus. Even after spending half a day on the road, he made it to the stadium before the event started.The clip did not include any dramatic reactions. It was a simple conversation between a father and son, and that is exactly what made it special for many viewers.IShowSpeed was already having a huge day after being announced as one of the performers in the FIFA World Cup 2026 closing ceremony before the final. FIFA included him in a lineup featuring several well-known names from music and entertainment.Still, the conversation with his father gave fans something different to remember.Over the years, Speed has shared many important moments from his career online. This time, though, the attention shifted to the person sitting beside him. His father’s story reminded people that big achievements are often backed by quiet sacrifices that happen away from cameras.The video also showed how proud his father was to witness the occasion in person.After dealing with a canceled flight and a long bus ride, he still arrived with a smile and made sure he was there before Speed stepped onto one of football’s biggest stages.For many fans, that simple story carried as much meaning as the performance itself.
Article Summary: Ishowspeed was announced as one of the performers in the fifa world cup 2026 closing ceremony before the final. A video showed the streamer sitting with his father inside the stadium before the closing ceremony. During the conversation, his dad revealed that he had to take a 12-hour bus ride after bad weather canceled his flight.

Article Title: Late drama! Enzo Fernandez sent off as Argentina reduced to 10 men before World Cup final extra time
Article Text:
Referee Slavko Vincic shows a red card to Argentina's Enzo Fernandez (24) during the World Cup final. (AP Photo)

Spain dominate but Martinez keeps Argentina alive



Historic goalless final reaches tense extra time



The FIFA World Cup 2026 final produced late drama before extra time as Argentina were forced to play the additional 30 minutes with 10 men after Enzo Fernandez was sent off in stoppage time against Spain.With the score locked at 0-0 after 90 minutes, Fernandez received a second yellow card in the 93rd minute following a late challenge on Spain defender Pau Cubarsi, leaving Lionel Messi 's side a man down at the most crucial stage of the title clash.The incident came moments after Argentina had survived another Spain attack. The ball had been cleared back upfield following an Emiliano Martinez save, but as Argentina attempted to launch their own move forward, Fernandez arrived late and caught Cubarsi with a reckless challenge.The referee had no hesitation in showing the midfielder a second yellow card, despite Fernandez's protests.Argentina were forced to defend the closing moments of normal time with 10 players and entered extra time facing an even greater challenge against a Spain side that had dominated possession throughout the match.Spain controlled the contest for large periods, producing a commanding display against the defending champions.La Roja finished regulation time with 15 shots compared to Argentina's zero, nine corners to Argentina's one, and overwhelming possession. Yet they were unable to find the breakthrough against an inspired Emiliano Martinez.The Argentina goalkeeper produced a record-breaking performance in the final, making 10 saves — the most ever in a World Cup final — to keep his team alive.One of his biggest interventions came immediately after Fernandez's dismissal when Spain teenager Lamine Yamal stepped up for a dangerous free-kick from around 22 yards. The 19-year-old's curling effort looked destined for the corner, but Martinez produced a stunning diving save to deny him.The goalkeeper immediately sprang to his feet, punching the air and urging the crowd to raise the noise inside the stadium.The deadlock after 90 minutes added another chapter to World Cup final history.The match was the ninth World Cup final to remain goalless at halftime, joining previous classics such as Spain vs Netherlands in 2010 and Germany vs Argentina in 2014.The opening half had already created a unique record, with just three total shots — the fewest in the first half of a World Cup final since records began in 1966.Argentina had also become the first team since 1966 to fail to register a first-half shot in any World Cup match.While Spain continued their push for a second World Cup title after their 2010 triumph, Argentina were chasing a fourth crown and hoping to become the first nation since Brazil in 1962 to successfully defend the trophy.
Article Summary: Enzo fernandez sent off in 93rd minute for late challenge on pau cubarsi. Referee slavko vincic had no hesitation in showing the midfielder a second yellow card. Argentina forced to defend closing moments of normal time with 10 players. Match was the ninth world cup final to remain goalless at halftime.

Article Title: FIFA WC final: Referee's accidental spray leaves Spain's Alex Baena wearing shaving cream in hilarious final blooper
Article Text:
Foam sprayed on Alex Baena (Photo/FIFA /X)

A Fresh Coat of Paint

Fans in Stitches

High-stakes World Cup finals are usually defined by tension, brilliant skill, and dramatic refereeing decisions. However, Sunday's grand finale between Spain and Argentina at MetLife Stadium provided a moment of pure, unscripted comedy that instantly broke the internet.Spanish midfielder Álex Baena found himself on the receiving end of a bizarre field mishap when match referee Slavko Vinčić accidentally blasted him square in the face with a generous dollop of vanishing spray.The hilarious incident unfolded in the heat of a tactical stoppage. As Vinčić reached down to mark out the ten-yard line for a free-kick wall, the Slovenian official misjudged his grip on the pressurized canister. Instead of painting a neat white line across the turf, the nozzle popped in the wrong direction, launching a thick cloud of white foam straight into Baena’s face and torso.For a brief second, the 24-year-old Spanish international stood frozen, looking like he had just signed up for a mid-match shaving cream commercial.The hilarious clip quickly exploded across social media platform X, racking up millions of views within minutes as global fans replayed the moment in slow motion.Commentators couldn't contain their giggles either, remarking that while referees usually issue yellow cards to cool down players, Vinčić found a far more literal way to frost the Spanish squad.Fortunately, both men handled the awkward moment with good humor. A visibly embarrassed Vinčić offered an immediate apology, helping the midfielder wipe off the white suds. Baena cracked a wide smile, wiped his eyes clean with his jersey, and quickly scampered back into position to resume the action, completely unharmed, albeit freshly white-washed for the global audience!
Article Summary: Referee slavko vinčić accidentally sprayed spanish midfielder alex baena with vanishing spray. The incident occurred during sunday's world cup final between spain and argentina at metlife stadium in new york. The hilarious clip quickly exploded across social media platform x, racking up millions of views within minutes.

Article Title: "Looks like teenage girls": Dave Portnoy's brutal verdict on BTS and Justin Bieber ignites FIFA World Cup controversy
Article Text:
Dave Portnoy sharply criticized the FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show, mocking BTS and questioning Justin Bieber's slow performance during football's biggest stage. While he praised Shakira and Burna Boy's energetic set, he felt the overall show missed the mark. His comments came as the inaugural World Cup halftime spectacle sparked widespread debate, with many fans divided over FIFA's new entertainment format.

Dave Portnoy slams FIFA World Cup 2026 halftime show and questions Justin Bieber 's performance

Donald Trump receives boos during FIFA World Cup 2026 final appearance



The FIFA World Cup 2026 final was expected to be remembered for football history, but the entertainment around the match quickly became one of the biggest talking points. While Spain's victory over Argentina closed the tournament, conversations soon shifted to the first-ever World Cup halftime show and the reaction that followed when US President Donald Trump arrived inside the stadium.Barstool Sports founder Dave Portnoy did not hide his disappointment after watching FIFA introduce a Super Bowl-style halftime show to the World Cup final. The performance featured global stars including BTS, Justin Bieber, Shakira and Burna Boy, with Coldplay frontman Chris Martin helping organize the event.Portnoy shared his thoughts almost immediately after the show ended, making it clear that several moments failed to impress him. He mocked BTS by saying they "looked like teenage girls," while also questioning the surprise appearance of Ted Lasso before Justin Bieber took the stage.His biggest criticism, however, was reserved for Bieber's song choice. Portnoy argued that a World Cup final should build excitement instead of slowing the atmosphere, saying he could not understand why the singer performed what he described as a "super slow jam" during one of football's biggest celebrations.Despite his criticism, Portnoy did find one highlight. He praised Shakira's performance of the official World Cup anthem, "Dai dai," alongside Burna Boy, suggesting that their set delivered the energy many viewers expected from such a global event.The halftime show has divided fans since it aired. Some welcomed FIFA's effort to introduce a new entertainment tradition, believing it could attract a wider international audience. Others felt football's biggest match should remain focused on the sport itself rather than celebrity performances. The mixed response has already fueled debate about whether FIFA should continue the concept when the tournament returns in 2030.The spotlight shifted once again before the trophy presentation when US President Donald Trump entered the field alongside FIFA president Gianni Infantino.Security around the venue had already been tightened because of Trump's attendance, leading to long queues outside the stadium. Once he appeared inside, many supporters responded with loud boos, although cheers could also be heard from parts of the crowd.The reaction quickly spread across social media, where fans shared videos and opinions from inside the stadium.One fan wrote, "Why is Donald Trump on my screen. Ew yuck."Another posted, "Donald Trump is booed by an entire stadium and keeps going as if nothing were happening. How does he manage it? Doesn't he have any sense of honour?"Journalist Adam Crafton also described what happened from the press box, writing, "You cannot hear much through the glass at MetLife Stadium in the press box but you could very much hear significant boos and whistles when U.S. president Donald Trump walked out alongside FIFA president Gianni Infantino."Another supporter added, "The entire bar I’m at is booing Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino."Trump's appearance had already drawn notice during the tournament, after he got involved in talks over Folarin Balogun's suspension and publicly predicted Lionel Messi would score the winning goal in the final.And when the final whistle sounded, the footballing story of the FIFA World Cup 2026 was filled with plenty of off-field drama, with the halftime show and Trump’s appearance sparking conversations.
Article Summary: Barstool sports founder dave portnoy sharply criticized the fifa world cup 2026 halftime show. While he praised shakira and burna boy's energetic set, he felt the overall show missed the mark. He mocked bts by saying they "looked like teenage girls," while also questioning the surprise appearance of ted lasso.

Article Title: Burnham is Labour chief, to take over as PM on July 20
Article Text:
Andy Burnham Andy Burnham Andy Burnham 1

2

LONDON: Andy Burnham promised to bring hope to the British people and purpose to the country's floundering Labour govt as he was officially declared party leader on Friday, clearing his final hurdle to take office as prime minister on July 20.The former mayor of Greater Manchester was the only contender in the centre-left party's leadership contest to replace departing PM Keir Starmer. Friday's announcement was a foregone conclusion after Burnham secured nominations from 379 of the 403 Labour lawmakers in the House of Commons."We're going to give them hope back," Burnham, 56, told an audience of lawmakers, party activists and trade union leaders in his first speech as leader. "This is a proud moment you have given me and my family today, and an emotional one, but it is one for which I am ready."He sketched out some priorities in Friday's speech, promising to deliver "hope in every heart" and "good growth in every postcode," in part by transferring power from central govt in London to local leaders in cities and regions. "We will take power back from Westminster and Whitehall and give it to the place you live," he told the audience."More power over life's essentials so you can make them work better."Starmer will remain prime minister until Monday, when he formally tenders his resignation to King Charles III. The king will then ask Burnham to form a govt. Britain's parliamentary democracy allows governing parties to change leaders, and thus prime ministers, without the need for a general election. The next national election doesn't have to be held until 2029. AP
Article Summary: Andy burnham was officially declared party leader on friday. The former mayor of greater manchester was the only contender in the contest. Burnham secured nominations from 379 of the 403 labour lawmakers in the house of commons. He will take office as prime minister on july 20.
